import json, pandas as pd
perfect = pd.DataFrame(var_call_TZcCJ4WqxoPVcIX0da1w76L4)
books_path = var_call_EpcojxWVaUkcb3DzU7UMlf6O
books = pd.read_json(books_path)
# filter English-language (details contains 'English') and Literature & Fiction category
books_eng = books[books['details'].str.contains('English', na=False)]
books_lit = books_eng[books_eng['categories'].str.contains('"Literature & Fiction"', na=False)]
# fuzzy join: assume purchaseid_X maps to bookid_X
perfect['book_id'] = perfect['purchase_id'].str.replace('purchaseid_','bookid_', regex=False)
merged = perfect.merge(books_lit, on='book_id', how='inner')
result = merged[['book_id','title','author','categories']].to_dict(orient='records')
out = json.dumps(result)
print('__RESULT__:')
print(out)

In Lebanon, he is still celebrated as a literary hero.\", \"He is chiefly known in the English-speaking world for his 1923 book The Prophet, an early example of inspirational fiction including a series of philosophical essays written in poetic English prose.
